scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from physical corruption/abuse and the right to have violators arrested is a bodily entitlement.
scores.generality_reasoning: Protection of minors is a broad legal category with wide application.
scores.persistence_reasoning: This resource is bounded to the life stage of being a 'youth' or minor.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age/youth is a biologically-fixed status at any given moment.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Aeschines describes a contemporary Athenian legal enforcement scenario.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim describes a specific incident of legal enforcement and the threat of the 'lock-up'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a youth does not inherently entail a specific legal right to have foreigners arrested.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to bring a suit is a concrete legal right with potential material payoffs.
scores.generality_reasoning: A specific legal procedure (bringing suit) for a specific sub-group (state slaves).
scores.persistence_reasoning: The status of being a state slave was typically long-term or lifelong.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slave vs. state slave is a structural legal birth-status, very hard to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to a known contemporary Athenian legal distinction.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim cites the different legal standing of ordinary vs. state slaves as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Freedom to sue is not part of the definition of 'slave' (usually the opposite).

scores.materiality_reasoning: Freedom from private ownership is a foundational material/bodily status.
scores.generality_reasoning: Protection from arbitrary enslavement is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Enslavement or freedom is a lifelong structural condition.
scores.immutability_reasoning: State slave status is a structural legal status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to a contemporary attempt at enslavement in Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a specific factual attempt to claim a public slave as private property.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a state slave does not define vulnerability to private claims.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from enslavement is a fundamental protection of the person and body.
scores.generality_reasoning: A foundational societal axis concerning legal personhood.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Avoidance of enslavement has lifelong consequences.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Status of 'resident' is changeable through movement but represents a durable identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes a contemporary legal settlement in Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim provides witness testimony of a specific legal event.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Residence does not inherently define legal protection from enslavement.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Regulation of the use of one's body for income has direct material effects.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad prohibition on a major economic and social activity for a large group.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The status of being an Athenian citizen is lifelong and structural.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Athenian citizenship is a hard-to-change legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The author cites laws 'read a few moments ago' in the trial.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Explicit citation of written laws and severe penalties as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Refraining from sex work is not part of the definition of an Athenian.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to hold office and vote (speaking in assembly) is a core legal right.
scores.generality_reasoning: Political participation is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Loss of political rights (atimia) was typically a permanent/structural change.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Athenian citizenship is a structural legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: This rule is the central legal pivot of the contemporary trial.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: References the charge as breaking specific existing laws.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being an Athenian does not by definition include or exclude prostitution.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to remain on citizen lists is the gateway to all other material rights.
scores.generality_reasoning: Encompasses the entirety of civic and legal personhood.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Citizenship is a lifelong, structural status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Athenian status is a structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes current, ongoing revisions of citizen-lists in demes.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes a standard, observed institutional procedure.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The rule essentially says those found not to be genuine citizens are excluded from citizenship.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from capital punishment affects bodily integrity and life itself.
scores.generality_reasoning: Bribery and its penalties represent a major legal-political category.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Death is a permanent, structural outcome.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Athenian citizenship is a structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Cites a 'measure of the following sort' by a known contemporary Demophilus.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites specific historical/recent punishments as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being an Athenian does not define one's susceptibility to the death penalty.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Two obols per day for support is a direct means of subsistence.
scores.generality_reasoning: A broad state-managed welfare provision.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Allowance persists as long as the infirmity and poverty last.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Severe physical infirmity/disability is a biologically-fixed trait.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Author cites a specific existing law in Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Direct citation of the law's specific criteria and dollar amounts.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Disability/poverty does not automatically define receiving a pension.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Managing an estate (property and land) is a tangible wealth entitlement.
scores.generality_reasoning: Right to inherit and control property is a foundation of the society.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Control of an estate holds for the duration of adulthood.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship status is a structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to Timarchus reaching legal majority in contemporary Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports Timarchus's legal assumption of control as factual history.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not define the act of inheriting an estate.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Earning money and conducting business provides tangible material wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Applies to a specific subset of skilled labor and a specific fee arrangement.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Work arrangements for skilled slaves could last many years.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slave status is a structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes common contemporary Athenian economic practice.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites specific daily fees and the term 'apophora' as a factual custom.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a slave does not inherently define the right to work for self-pay.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Liturgy obligations involve the direct expenditure of significant wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Liturgies were a major systemic structure for financing the Athenian state.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Obligations recurred as long as the wealth bracket was maintained.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth bracket is changeable but serves as a durable social identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Directly describes contemporary Athenian fiscal obligations.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites specific workshops and land sales to avoid factual legal burdens.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being wealthy does not define a legal requirement to fund trierarchies.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Legal audits of officials produce tangible outcomes like fines or exemptions.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational constitutional check across all public offices.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The audit occurs once at the close of an office term.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Public officer is a temporary, term-limited role.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the existing Athenian constitution.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the board of auditors as a factual institution.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Holding office does not inherently define undergoing an audit (though it was law).

scores.materiality_reasoning: Council membership is access to a social role with material political payoffs.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific institution (the Senate) and its procedural rules.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Resource held for the one-year duration of Senate service.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship (required for Senate) is structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes a specific contemporary procedure in the Athenian Senate.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes a dual-ballot procedure as standard practice.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not define the possibility of expulsion from the Senate.

scores.materiality_reasoning: A crown or garland is primarily a symbolic/ceremonial honor.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to a specific honor for a specific office.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The honor is awarded annually at the close of service.

scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes a habitual practice of the Assembly rewarding the Senate.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The avoidance of the death penalty directly concerns physical existence.
scores.generality_reasoning: Applies to a specific legal scenario (admitted theft).
scores.persistence_reasoning: The preservation of life is a lifelong outcome.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Citizenship status is a legal right that grants broad material access.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to address the assembly is a central political entitlement.

scores.persistence_reasoning: The penalty of atimia was typically permanent.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The rule targets 'men' (sex/gender), which is biologically fixed.
